title: Implement predicate evaluation engine and routing table
purpose: Implements the predicate evaluation engine and the routing table — the runtime machinery that makes predicate subscriptions work. The evaluation engine matches predicates against borrowed message fields with zero per-message allocation. The routing table maps channels to active subscriptions and supports concurrent updates without blocking evaluation. Together, these deliver the common-case routing path described in ADR-003.
task: Create the evaluation engine in routing/evaluate.rs and the routing table in routing/table.rs. The evaluator takes a Predicate and a borrowed field accessor, returning a bool. It must short-circuit AND at first false and OR at first true, and must not allocate on the evaluation hot path. The routing table maps channel identifiers to subscription sets. Subscription registration and removal must be concurrent-safe (readers proceed while writers update). Publishing to a channel with no subscriptions completes without error. This brief does not implement predicate compilation or routing functions — those belong to ROUTING-003.

requirements.title: Implement zero-allocation predicate evaluation against borrowed fields
requirements.spec: WHEN a Predicate is evaluated against a message field accessor, THE SYSTEM SHALL match comparison operators against field values by borrowing message fields without heap allocation on the hot path. WHEN a referenced field is missing from the message, THE SYSTEM SHALL return false without error. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allocate heap memory during predicate evaluation for any predicate variant.
acceptance: evaluate(Predicate::Comparison { field: "amount", op: Gt, value: Integer(1000) }, accessor) returns true when accessor yields Integer(1500) for "amount".
evaluate(Predicate::Comparison { field: "amount", op: Gt, value: Integer(1000) }, accessor) returns false when accessor yields Integer(500) for "amount".
evaluate(Predicate::Exists { field: "region" }, accessor) returns true when accessor yields a value for "region".
evaluate(Predicate::Exists { field: "missing" }, accessor) returns false when accessor yields None for "missing".
evaluate(Predicate::Comparison { field: "missing", op: Eq, value: Text("x") }, accessor) returns false without error when field is absent.
The evaluate function signature takes references only: fn evaluate(predicate: &Predicate, accessor: &dyn FieldAccessor) -> bool.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:1-180 — new evaluator module target; define `FieldAccessor`, a borrowed field-value view if needed, `evaluate(&Predicate, &dyn FieldAccessor) -> bool`, and local tests here.
crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:1-86 — existing `ComparisonOp`, owned `FieldValue`, `FieldPath`, and `Predicate` variants that the evaluator must match without cloning.

crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs:1-3 — currently exports only `predicate`; add `pub mod evaluate;` and re-export `evaluate`, `FieldAccessor`, and any borrowed value type while keeping CN3 export-only.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace lints deny unsafe, all/pedantic clippy issues, `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, and `panic`; tests and helpers need to be written accordingly.
docs/design/routing/design.json:49-58 — CN4/CN5 constraints: public types need `Debug`; predicate evaluation must avoid per-message heap allocation.
context: `FieldValue::Text(String)` is owned because predicates own literal values; do not clone those strings or build new `String`s while evaluating. Compare by borrowing (`as_str`) against a borrowed message-field representation.

There is no existing `FieldAccessor`; introduce it in `evaluate.rs`. To support messages that do not already store `FieldValue`, prefer a borrowed view such as `FieldValueRef<'a> { Text(&'a str), Integer(i64), Float(f64), Boolean(bool), Null }` returned from the accessor, rather than forcing accessors to allocate `FieldValue::Text(String)` on lookup.

Missing fields are not errors: `Comparison` and all other field-dependent variants should return `false` immediately when `accessor.field(field)` returns `None`; `Exists` is the only variant whose result is just presence/absence.

For comparison operators, keep type mismatches false rather than trying to coerce text/numeric/bool/null values; for floats, avoid unwraps around `partial_cmp` so NaN or unordered values simply evaluate false for ordering.
scout.approach: Create `routing/evaluate.rs` with a `pub trait FieldAccessor: std::fmt::Debug` whose lookup method borrows from the message, plus a public borrowed value enum if the accessor cannot safely return `&FieldValue`. Implement `pub fn evaluate(predicate: &Predicate, accessor: &dyn FieldAccessor) -> bool` as a single `match` over `Predicate`; for `Comparison`, fetch the field once, return false on `None`, and dispatch to small private helpers like `compare_values(field_value, *op, literal)` that compare same-type values by reference. Add focused tests for integer greater-than true/false, `Exists` true/false, and missing comparison false; tests can allocate while constructing predicates/messages, but the `evaluate` implementation itself should contain no `Vec::new`, `collect`, `String::from`, `to_string`, `clone` of `String`, `Box::new`, etc. on the hot path.
scout.notes: `FieldPath` currently exposes only `segments()` and keeps its inner string private. In test accessors, avoid `FieldPath::new("amount")` inside each lookup because that allocates; either match using `path.segments().eq(["amount"])` or add a tiny borrowed `FieldPath::as_str()` only if the implementer accepts the small out-of-brief predicate.rs change.

files_changed.note: Added FieldAccessor, FieldValueRef, evaluate(&Predicate, &dyn FieldAccessor) -> bool, same-type comparison helpers, missing-field false behavior, and R1 acceptance tests.
Re-exported evaluate, FieldAccessor, and FieldValueRef from the routing module root while keeping mod.rs export-only.

dev.how: Created a borrowed field-value view and a Debug-bounded FieldAccessor trait so message fields can be matched without constructing owned FieldValue values on lookup. The evaluator fetches referenced fields once, returns false for missing fields, compares text by &str and numeric/bool/null values directly, treats type mismatches as false, and keeps the public signature exactly reference-based: evaluate(predicate: &Predicate, accessor: &dyn FieldAccessor) -> bool.

checklist.note: Predicate evaluation matches against borrowed FieldValueRef values and the evaluate hot path performs no heap allocation.

stories.note: Predicate subscriptions such as amount > 1000 and region equality can now be evaluated in the bus without application-side filtering.

acceptance.criterion: evaluate(Predicate::Comparison { field: "amount", op: Gt, value: Integer(1000) }, accessor) returns true when accessor yields Integer(1500) for "amount".
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:30-32 dispatches Comparison through accessor.field(...).is_some_and(compare_values); integer comparison uses left.cmp(right) at lines 53-55. Test routing::evaluate::tests::integer_greater_than_comparison_matches covers amount=1500 > 1000 at lines 168-174.

acceptance.criterion: evaluate(Predicate::Comparison { field: "amount", op: Gt, value: Integer(1000) }, accessor) returns false when accessor yields Integer(500) for "amount".
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:53-55 and 67-75 make Gt depend on Ordering::is_gt. Test routing::evaluate::tests::integer_greater_than_comparison_rejects_lower_value covers amount=500 at lines 176-182.

acceptance.criterion: evaluate(Predicate::Exists { field: "region" }, accessor) returns true when accessor yields a value for "region".
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:44 returns accessor.field(field).is_some() for Exists. Test routing::evaluate::tests::exists_returns_true_for_present_field covers present region at lines 184-192.

acceptance.criterion: evaluate(Predicate::Exists { field: "missing" }, accessor) returns false when accessor yields None for "missing".
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:44 returns false on None. Test routing::evaluate::tests::exists_returns_false_for_missing_field covers missing field at lines 194-202.

acceptance.criterion: evaluate(Predicate::Comparison { field: "missing", op: Eq, value: Text("x") }, accessor) returns false without error when field is absent.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:30-32 uses Option::is_some_and, so an absent field returns false and no Result/error path exists. Test routing::evaluate::tests::comparison_returns_false_for_missing_field covers the missing Text comparison at lines 204-214.

acceptance.criterion: The evaluate function signature takes references only: fn evaluate(predicate: &Predicate, accessor: &dyn FieldAccessor) -> bool.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:28 defines `pub fn evaluate(predicate: &Predicate, accessor: &dyn FieldAccessor) -> bool`; FieldAccessor returns borrowed FieldValueRef at lines 20-24.

requirements.title: Implement short-circuit evaluation for boolean combinators
requirements.spec: WHEN evaluating a Predicate::And, THE SYSTEM SHALL stop at the first predicate that evaluates to false and return false immediately. WHEN evaluating a Predicate::Or, THE SYSTEM SHALL stop at the first predicate that evaluates to true and return true immediately. WHEN evaluating a Predicate::Not, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the logical negation. Empty And SHALL evaluate to true (vacuous truth). Empty Or SHALL evaluate to false.
acceptance: And([true_pred, false_pred, never_evaluated_pred]) returns false and does not evaluate the third predicate.
Or([false_pred, true_pred, never_evaluated_pred]) returns true and does not evaluate the third predicate.
Not(true_pred) returns false; Not(false_pred) returns true.
And([]) returns true (vacuous truth).
Or([]) returns false.
Short-circuit behavior is verifiable via a counting field accessor that tracks access count.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:1-220 — extend the R1 evaluator with `And`, `Or`, and `Not` arms plus counting-accessor tests.
crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:66-71 — boolean combinator shapes are `And(Vec<Self>)`, `Or(Vec<Self>)`, and `Not(Box<Self>)`; preserve these semantics without adding depth limits.
docs/design/routing/checklist.json:15-22 — C7 explicitly requires short-circuit `AND`/`OR`; C8-C10 are compiler work and should not be pulled into this brief.
docs/design/routing/stories.json:5-13 — S2 frames the user goal: arbitrary declarative composition without writing a routing function.
context: Rust iterator methods `all` and `any` already implement the required short-circuit behavior and naturally make `And([])` true and `Or([])` false; `fold`, `map(...).collect()`, or precomputing child booleans would violate the acceptance criteria.

A counting accessor should count actual field lookups, not predicate nodes. Build test predicates whose third child references a field that would increment the counter if evaluated, then assert the count stops before that child.

`Not` should recursively call `evaluate` exactly once on its child and negate the result. Do not special-case nested boolean expressions or introduce a max recursion/composition depth; ROUTING-001 explicitly allowed arbitrary nesting.
Keep boolean tests in `evaluate.rs` rather than `predicate.rs`; `predicate.rs` is only the type-definition module from ROUTING-001.
scout.approach: Add `Predicate::And(children) => children.iter().all(|child| evaluate(child, accessor))`, `Predicate::Or(children) => children.iter().any(|child| evaluate(child, accessor))`, and `Predicate::Not(child) => !evaluate(child, accessor)` to the evaluator match. For tests, define small helper predicates over existing/missing fields and a `#[derive(Debug)]` accessor with `Cell<usize>` or atomics to count lookups; verify `And([true, false, third])` stops before `third`, `Or([false, true, third])` stops before `third`, `Not` negates, and empty `And`/`Or` return the vacuous values. Avoid sentinel `panic!` accessors because clippy's panic lint is denied across all targets.
scout.notes: Short-circuit tests can accidentally pass if the first two predicates do not touch fields. Make each evaluated predicate perform a lookup so the counter proves both positive evaluation and non-evaluation of the trailing child.

files_changed.note: Added And, Or, and Not evaluator arms plus counting-accessor tests proving short-circuit behavior and empty-combinator semantics.

dev.how: Implemented boolean combinator evaluation with children.iter().all(...) for AND and children.iter().any(...) for OR, preserving Rust's short-circuit semantics and yielding vacuous truth for empty AND and false for empty OR. NOT recursively evaluates exactly one child and negates the result; tests use a Cell-backed counting accessor to verify the trailing child is not looked up.

checklist.note: AND stops at the first false child and OR stops at the first true child.

stories.note: Developers can compose declarative predicates with AND, OR, and NOT without writing routing functions.

acceptance.criterion: And([true_pred, false_pred, never_evaluated_pred]) returns false and does not evaluate the third predicate.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:33 uses children.iter().all(...), which short-circuits on false. Test routing::evaluate::tests::and_short_circuits_at_first_false asserts false and count==2 at lines 216-227.

acceptance.criterion: Or([false_pred, true_pred, never_evaluated_pred]) returns true and does not evaluate the third predicate.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:34 uses children.iter().any(...), which short-circuits on true. Test routing::evaluate::tests::or_short_circuits_at_first_true asserts true and count==2 at lines 229-240.

acceptance.criterion: Not(true_pred) returns false; Not(false_pred) returns true.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:35 returns `!evaluate(child, accessor)`. Test routing::evaluate::tests::not_negates_child_predicate covers both outcomes at lines 242-250.

acceptance.criterion: And([]) returns true (vacuous truth).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:33 delegates to Iterator::all, whose empty result is true; test routing::evaluate::tests::empty_boolean_combinators_have_vacuous_values asserts Predicate::And(Vec::new()) is true at lines 252-257.

acceptance.criterion: Or([]) returns false.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:34 delegates to Iterator::any, whose empty result is false; test routing::evaluate::tests::empty_boolean_combinators_have_vacuous_values asserts Predicate::Or(Vec::new()) is false at lines 258-261.

acceptance.criterion: Short-circuit behavior is verifiable via a counting field accessor that tracks access count.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:111-142 defines CountingAccessor with a Cell-backed count incremented on field access; short-circuit tests assert count==2 at lines 225-226 and 238-239.

requirements.title: Implement range predicate evaluation
requirements.spec: WHEN evaluating a Predicate::Range, THE SYSTEM SHALL return true if the field value is greater than or equal to the lower bound AND less than or equal to the upper bound (inclusive on both ends). WHEN the field is missing, THE SYSTEM SHALL return false. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return an error for type-mismatched range comparisons; they evaluate to false.
acceptance: Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns true for Integer(150).
Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns true for Integer(100) (lower inclusive).
Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns true for Integer(200) (upper inclusive).
Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns false for Integer(50).
Range against a missing field returns false.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:1-240 — implement the `Predicate::Range` arm and helper comparisons in the same evaluator module.
crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:72-80 — existing `Range { field, lower, upper }` type states inclusive bounds for numeric or text values.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-002.json:54-70 — exact range acceptance: inclusive lower/upper, missing field false, type mismatch false.
docs/design/routing/checklist.json:5-22 — C3 was type support from ROUTING-001; ROUTING-002 must now make range evaluation consistent with C6/C7 allocation constraints.
context: Fetch the field once for a range predicate. Do not evaluate `lower` and `upper` by re-querying the accessor or by constructing intermediate predicates that might allocate.
Inclusive range can share comparison helpers: `gte(field, lower) && lte(field, upper)`, but ensure the helper handles same-type values without allocating and returns false for mismatches.

Text ranges should compare borrowed `&str` lexicographically against literal `String::as_str()`. Integer and float ranges should use numeric comparison; bool/null should not be ordered unless explicitly added later.

The spec explicitly says type-mismatched ranges evaluate false without error; tests should include at least missing field false and, ideally, an integer range against a text value false to lock that behavior down.
scout.approach: In the `Range` match arm, call the accessor once, return false on `None`, then check the borrowed field value against both bounds inclusively using private helpers such as `value_gte(field, lower) && value_lte(field, upper)`. Implement same-type comparisons for `Integer`, `Float`, and `Text`; use direct float operators or `partial_cmp().is_some_and(...)` so NaN/unordered comparisons are false without unwraps. Add acceptance tests for 150, 100, 200, 50, and missing amount; add one mismatch regression test if space permits because it is a likely future bug.
scout.notes: Do not add exclusive-bound variants, bound-order validation, or SQL-like `BETWEEN` parsing here. The predicate type is already fixed and parser/compiler work belongs to later routing briefs.

files_changed.note: Added inclusive Range evaluation using the same borrowed comparison helpers and tests for middle, lower bound, upper bound, below-lower, missing field, and type mismatch cases.

dev.how: Implemented Predicate::Range by fetching the target field once and checking it is greater than or equal to the lower bound and less than or equal to the upper bound. Same-type integer, float, and text ranges evaluate inclusively; missing fields and type mismatches return false without errors, and unordered float comparisons remain false via partial_cmp handling.

acceptance.criterion: Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns true for Integer(150).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:36-43 evaluates Range as Gte lower && Lte upper. Test routing::evaluate::tests::range_includes_middle_and_bounds iterates value 150 at lines 264-270.

acceptance.criterion: Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns true for Integer(100) (lower inclusive).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:41 compares with ComparisonOp::Gte for the lower bound. Test routing::evaluate::tests::range_includes_middle_and_bounds includes value 100 at lines 264-270.

acceptance.criterion: Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns true for Integer(200) (upper inclusive).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:42 compares with ComparisonOp::Lte for the upper bound. Test routing::evaluate::tests::range_includes_middle_and_bounds includes value 200 at lines 264-270.

acceptance.criterion: Range { field: "amount", lower: Integer(100), upper: Integer(200) } returns false for Integer(50).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:41-42 requires both lower and upper comparisons. Test routing::evaluate::tests::range_rejects_value_below_lower_bound covers amount=50 at lines 273-278.

acceptance.criterion: Range against a missing field returns false.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:40 wraps the range comparison in accessor.field(field).is_some_and(...), so None is false. Test routing::evaluate::tests::range_rejects_missing_field covers missing amount at lines 280-285.

requirements.title: Define routing table mapping channels to subscription sets
requirements.spec: The routing module SHALL define a RoutingTable that maps channel identifiers to sets of active subscriptions. Each subscription SHALL associate a subscriber identity with a Predicate. The RoutingTable SHALL support resolving all matching subscriptions for a given channel and message. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT impose a hard limit on subscriptions per channel.
acceptance: RoutingTable::new() creates an empty table.
After registering a subscription with a predicate on channel "orders", resolve("orders", message) returns the subscription when the predicate matches.
resolve("orders", message) returns an empty set when no subscriptions match.
Multiple subscriptions on the same channel are independently evaluated.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:1-240 — new routing table target; define `RoutingTable`, subscription identity/types, register/remove/resolve APIs, and table tests.
crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:1-180 — table resolution should call the evaluator against the caller-supplied `FieldAccessor` rather than reimplementing predicate matching.
crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs:1-3 — add `pub mod table;` and re-export the public table/subscription types while preserving the export-only module root.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-34 — nearby public type style: documented fields, small `new` constructors, `#[must_use]`, and `Clone/Debug/PartialEq` where appropriate.
docs/design/routing/design.json:32-39 — intended routing module layout includes `evaluate.rs` and `table.rs`; compiler/function/dispatch/group remain later briefs.
context: No subscriber identity type exists yet; define a small public newtype such as `SubscriberId(String)` plus `Subscription { subscriber: SubscriberId, predicate: Predicate }`. Derive `Debug` on all public types per CN4; `Subscription` can derive `PartialEq` but not `Eq` because `Predicate` contains `FieldValue::Float(f64)`.
The repository currently uses std collections (`BTreeMap` in metrics) and no `dashmap`/`arc-swap` dependency in `crates/liminal/Cargo.toml`; a std-only table is the least invasive approach.

Although C16 mentions predicate or function subscriptions, this brief's boundaries forbid routing functions. Do not add placeholder function variants or function execution hooks; store predicate subscriptions only and leave enum widening to ROUTING-003 if needed.
Use an unbounded collection (`Vec`, `BTreeMap`, or `Arc<[...]>`) with no artificial constant limit. A `BTreeMap<String, ...>` for channels matches existing deterministic collection style.
scout.approach: Create `RoutingTable` as a cloneable `Arc`-backed handle around an inner state keyed by channel name. Define `Subscription::new(subscriber, predicate)` and register subscriptions on a channel, then implement `resolve(channel, accessor)` by taking a short snapshot of that channel's active subscriptions and evaluating each subscription's predicate with `evaluate`. Prefer returning `Vec<Arc<Subscription>>` (or another owned match set) so results outlive the table lock; tests should cover empty `RoutingTable::new()`, one matching `orders` subscription, no matches, and multiple subscriptions on the same channel being evaluated independently.
scout.notes: The acceptance text says “Ok with an empty set,” but the current evaluator has no error path and the workspace denies clippy `unnecessary_wraps`. A direct empty `Vec` is cleaner unless the implementer introduces a real routing-table error; if a `Result` API is chosen for integration reasons, zero matches must be `Ok(Vec::new())`, never an error.

files_changed.note: Added RoutingTable, SubscriberId, Subscription, registration/removal/resolve APIs, and routing-table behavior tests.
Re-exported RoutingTable, SubscriberId, and Subscription from the routing module root.

dev.how: Defined a cloneable Arc-backed RoutingTable keyed by channel name, with each active Subscription associating a SubscriberId and Predicate. resolve(channel, accessor) snapshots the channel's subscription set, evaluates each predicate with the evaluator, and returns owned Arc<Subscription> handles for every match with no hard subscription limit.

checklist.note: RoutingTable maps channels to active predicate subscriptions and resolves all matching subscriptions for a channel/message.

stories.note: A developer can register a predicate subscription on a channel and have the table return only matching subscribers.

acceptance.criterion: RoutingTable::new() creates an empty table.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:52-59 constructs TableInner with default state, and is_empty reads channels.is_empty at lines 61-65. Test routing::table::tests::new_table_is_empty asserts this at lines 269-274.

acceptance.criterion: After registering a subscription with a predicate on channel "orders", resolve("orders", message) returns the subscription when the predicate matches.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:67-90 registers an Arc<Subscription> under the channel; resolve filters by evaluate and returns cloned matching Arc handles at lines 124-140. Test routing::table::tests::resolve_returns_matching_subscription covers orders amount=1500 at lines 276-288.

acceptance.criterion: resolve("orders", message) returns an empty set when no subscriptions match.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:136-140 collects only predicates for which evaluate returns true, so all-false yields an empty Vec. Test routing::table::tests::resolve_returns_empty_when_no_subscription_matches asserts empty at lines 290-300.

acceptance.criterion: Multiple subscriptions on the same channel are independently evaluated.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:136-140 iterates every subscription in the channel snapshot independently. Test routing::table::tests::multiple_subscriptions_on_channel_are_evaluated_independently registers low/high subscriptions and returns only the low match at lines 302-313.

requirements.title: Implement concurrent-safe subscription management
requirements.spec: WHEN a subscription is registered or removed, THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT block concurrent routing evaluations on the same table. THE SYSTEM SHALL use a read-write synchronization strategy that allows multiple concurrent readers during subscription updates.
acceptance: Subscription registration returns without blocking a concurrent resolve call on a different thread.
Subscription removal returns without blocking a concurrent resolve call.
The RoutingTable is Send + Sync.
Registering a subscription during an active resolve does not corrupt the table state.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:1-300 — implement concurrent-safe register/remove/resolve and tests here; the critical design is snapshot-before-evaluate.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:111-121 — existing cloneable handle pattern using `Arc<RegistryInner>` for shared registries.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:292-300 — existing `RwLock`-protected inner state pattern to mirror for table state.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:402-413 — existing poison handling helpers recover with `poisoned.into_inner()` instead of panicking or returning lock errors.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-17 — current dependencies do not include lock-free maps; prefer `std::sync::{Arc, RwLock}` unless a dependency addition is explicitly justified.
context: Do not hold an `RwLockReadGuard` while evaluating predicates. A slow accessor or many subscriptions would make registration/removal wait for the full resolve, failing the “does not block concurrent routing evaluations” intent.

A good std-only pattern is copy-on-write snapshots: store each channel's subscriptions behind an `Arc<[Arc<Subscription>]>`; `resolve` clones the slice `Arc` under a read lock and drops the lock before iterating, while register/remove take a write lock only long enough to replace the channel snapshot.

`RoutingTable` will be `Send + Sync` automatically if its fields are `Arc<RwLock<...>>` and stored subscription/accessor-independent types are Send+Sync. Add a static assertion helper in tests, e.g. `fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() {}` then call it for `RoutingTable`.

Concurrent tests should avoid sleeps as proof of non-blocking. Use `Barrier`, atomics, or channels to hold an accessor inside `resolve` after the snapshot has been taken, then register/remove from another thread and assert the updater completes before the accessor is released.
scout.approach: Implement `RoutingTable` with an inner `RwLock<TableState>` and helper functions `read_table_state`/`write_table_state` matching metrics poison recovery. For register/remove, clone the existing channel snapshot into a `Vec<Arc<Subscription>>`, mutate that vector, convert it back into `Arc<[Arc<Subscription>]>`, and swap it into the map; for resolve, clone the channel snapshot under a read lock, drop the guard, then evaluate against that immutable snapshot. This satisfies concurrent readers and prevents active resolves from observing corrupted state while keeping updates independent of long predicate/accessor work.
scout.notes: A plain `RwLock<BTreeMap<String, Vec<Subscription>>>` where `resolve` iterates under the read lock is the trap: it is easy to write and passes simple single-thread tests, but registration/removal will block behind active resolves and fail R5.

files_changed.note: Implemented RwLock-protected table state with copy-on-write Arc slice snapshots, poison recovery helpers, Send+Sync assertion, and concurrent register/remove/resolve tests.

dev.how: Used an Arc<RwLock<TableState>> handle and stored each channel's subscriptions as Arc<[Arc<Subscription>]>. register and remove take the write lock only long enough to replace a channel snapshot; resolve clones the Arc slice under a read lock and drops the guard before evaluating predicates, so active resolution work does not block updates and in-flight resolves see a consistent snapshot.

checklist.note: Subscription registration/removal swap snapshots without holding the table lock during routing evaluation, and RoutingTable is Send + Sync.

acceptance.criterion: Subscription registration returns without blocking a concurrent resolve call on a different thread.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:124-130 clones the channel snapshot under the read lock and drops the guard before predicate evaluation; test routing::table::tests::register_does_not_block_active_resolve starts a resolve blocked inside the accessor, registers concurrently, then releases the resolver at lines 320-341.

acceptance.criterion: Subscription removal returns without blocking a concurrent resolve call.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:92-122 removes by replacing the channel snapshot while resolve evaluation holds only its Arc snapshot; test routing::table::tests::remove_does_not_block_active_resolve removes while the resolver is blocked inside the accessor at lines 343-367.

acceptance.criterion: The RoutingTable is Send + Sync.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:46-50 defines RoutingTable as Arc<TableInner> over RwLock state, and test routing::table::tests::routing_table_is_send_and_sync uses assert_send_sync::<RoutingTable>() at lines 247 and 315-318.

acceptance.criterion: Registering a subscription during an active resolve does not corrupt the table state.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:77-88 copy-on-writes the channel Arc slice, and resolve uses a cloned snapshot at lines 127-140. Test routing::table::tests::register_during_active_resolve asserts the active resolve sees one subscription and the final table has initial/new in order at lines 369-393.

requirements.title: Handle publish to channels with no matching subscriptions
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is published to a channel with no registered subscriptions, THE SYSTEM SHALL complete the resolve operation without error, returning an empty match set. WHEN a channel has subscriptions but none match the message, THE SYSTEM SHALL return an empty match set without error.
acceptance: resolve("nonexistent-channel", message) returns Ok with an empty set.
resolve("orders", message) returns Ok with an empty set when all subscription predicates evaluate to false.
No error type is produced for zero-match scenarios.

files: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:1-320 — resolve behavior for nonexistent channels and no matching subscriptions belongs in the table implementation and tests.
crates/liminal/src/routing/evaluate.rs:1-220 — false predicate results should be normal boolean outcomes, not errors propagated to table resolution.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — existing crate-wide error taxonomy includes subscription/publish errors, but zero-match routing should not add or reuse an error variant.
docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-002.json:109-123 — exact R6 acceptance: nonexistent channel and all-nonmatching channel return an empty match set without error.
context: Missing channel and no matching subscriptions are successful resolution outcomes. Do not map them to `LiminalError::ChannelNotFound`, `SubscriptionFailed`, or a new `RoutingTableError`.
If a channel entry is removed when its last subscription is removed, `resolve` should treat the absent map key the same as an existing empty subscription slice: return an empty match set.
Filtering all subscriptions with `evaluate` naturally handles the “subscriptions exist but none match” case. The table should not special-case false predicates as failures or log noisy errors.

The return collection should be freshly owned by the caller; never return references tied to a lock guard just to avoid allocation, because that would either fail lifetimes or require holding the lock beyond resolution.
scout.approach: Make `resolve` start by snapshotting `state.channels.get(channel).cloned()`; if that returns `None`, immediately return an empty match collection. If a snapshot exists, iterate over it, clone only the matching `Arc<Subscription>` handles into the result, and return the result even when it remains empty. Add two explicit tests: resolving `nonexistent-channel` on an empty table, and resolving `orders` after registering a predicate that does not match the provided accessor; both should assert an empty successful result and no error construction.
scout.notes: This requirement is where an over-eager API can create reviewer friction: `ChannelNotFound` sounds plausible, but the design says publishing to a channel with no matching subscriptions completes normally for routing purposes.

files_changed.note: Added successful empty-match behavior for nonexistent channels and nonmatching subscriptions, with explicit tests.

dev.how: resolve treats absent channel entries and all-false predicate results as normal successful outcomes by returning an empty Vec<Arc<Subscription>>. No routing-table error type was added or reused for zero-match scenarios, and removing the last subscription removes the channel entry while preserving the same empty resolution behavior.

checklist.note: Publishing/resolving against channels with no matching subscriptions completes normally with an empty match set and no error path.

acceptance.criterion: resolve("nonexistent-channel", message) returns Ok with an empty set.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:126 returns Vec<Arc<Subscription>> with no error type; lines 132-134 return Vec::new() for absent channels. Test routing::table::tests::resolve_nonexistent_channel_returns_empty_set asserts empty at lines 395-401.

acceptance.criterion: resolve("orders", message) returns Ok with an empty set when all subscription predicates evaluate to false.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:136-140 filters unmatched subscriptions out and returns an empty Vec with no error path. Test routing::table::tests::resolve_returns_empty_when_no_subscription_matches asserts empty for amount=500 at lines 290-300.

acceptance.criterion: No error type is produced for zero-match scenarios.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:124-126 declares `pub fn resolve(...) -> Vec<Arc<Subscription>>`; no Result or routing-table error type is involved for zero-match cases, and absent channels return Vec::new() at lines 132-134.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement predicate-to-function compilation — that belongs to ROUTING-003.
SHALL NOT implement routing function loading or execution — that belongs to ROUTING-003.
SHALL NOT implement dispatch conversation logic — that belongs to ROUTING-004.
SHALL NOT define backpressure signals or capacity types — those belong to ROUTING-005.
SHALL NOT persist routing table state — table is reconstructed from consumer re-registration per design non-goals.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean.
cargo test -p liminal passes with all evaluation and routing table tests green.
evaluate.rs and table.rs are each under 500 lines including tests (CN1).
No heap allocation in evaluate() hot path — verify no Vec::new(), Box::new(), String::from() in evaluation code paths.
RoutingTable is Send + Sync — verified by static assertion or test.
